prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(initialize_task vehicle_1)
(initialize_task driver_1)
(initialize_task route_plan_1)
(initialize_task route_plan_2)
(assign_transport_mode vehicle_1 mode_van)
(assign_transport_mode driver_1 mode_bike)
(assign_transport_mode route_plan_1 mode_e_cargo)
(assign_dispatch_window vehicle_1 dispatch_window_1)
(activate_entity vehicle_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_handling_agent vehicle_1 handling_agent_1)
(signal_origin_zone_ready vehicle_1 origin_zone_1 dispatch_window_1)
(stage_vehicle_at_origin vehicle_1 origin_zone_1 handling_agent_1)
(release_handling_agent vehicle_1 handling_agent_1)
(release_dispatch_window vehicle_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_dispatch_window driver_1 dispatch_window_1)
(activate_entity driver_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_handling_agent driver_1 handling_agent_1)
(signal_destination_zone_ready driver_1 destination_zone_1 dispatch_window_1)
(stage_driver_at_destination driver_1 destination_zone_1 handling_agent_1)
(release_handling_agent driver_1 handling_agent_1)
(release_dispatch_window driver_1 dispatch_window_1)
(reserve_transport_leg vehicle_1 driver_1 origin_zone_1 destination_zone_1 transport_leg_1)
(assign_dispatch_window vehicle_1 dispatch_window_1)
(activate_leg_for_loading transport_leg_1 vehicle_1 dispatch_window_1)
(release_dispatch_window vehicle_1 dispatch_window_1)
(confirm_vehicle_deployment vehicle_1 transport_leg_1)
(confirm_driver_deployment driver_1 transport_leg_1)
(assign_dispatch_window route_plan_1 dispatch_window_1)
(activate_entity route_plan_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_and_materialize_container route_plan_1 container_1 transport_leg_1)
(mark_route_container_ready route_plan_1 container_1 transport_leg_1 dispatch_window_1)
(release_dispatch_window route_plan_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_vehicle_equipment_to_route route_plan_1 equipment_1)
(assign_handling_agent route_plan_1 handling_agent_1)
(prepare_route_for_compliance_without_alternate route_plan_1 equipment_1 handling_agent_1 container_1 transport_leg_1)
(assign_regulatory_clearance_to_route route_plan_1 clearance_1)
(apply_route_clearance route_plan_1 clearance_1 container_1 transport_leg_1)
(finalize_route_without_service_requirements route_plan_1)
(release_vehicle_equipment_from_route route_plan_1 equipment_1)
(release_route_clearance route_plan_1 clearance_1)
(release_handling_agent route_plan_1 handling_agent_1)
(assign_dispatch_window vehicle_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_entity_to_delivery_slot vehicle_1 delivery_slot_1 dispatch_window_1)
(deploy_vehicle_and_release_resources vehicle_1 mode_van delivery_slot_1)
(release_dispatch_window vehicle_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_dispatch_window driver_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_entity_to_delivery_slot driver_1 delivery_slot_1 dispatch_window_1)
(deploy_driver_and_release_resources driver_1 mode_bike delivery_slot_1)
(release_dispatch_window driver_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_dispatch_window route_plan_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_entity_to_delivery_slot route_plan_1 delivery_slot_1 dispatch_window_1)
(deploy_route_and_release_resources route_plan_1 mode_e_cargo delivery_slot_1)
(release_dispatch_window route_plan_1 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_transport_mode route_plan_2 mode_van)
(assign_dispatch_window route_plan_2 dispatch_window_1)
(activate_entity route_plan_2 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_and_materialize_container route_plan_2 container_2 transport_leg_1)
(mark_route_container_ready route_plan_2 container_2 transport_leg_1 dispatch_window_1)
(release_dispatch_window route_plan_2 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_vehicle_equipment_to_route route_plan_2 equipment_1)
(assign_handling_agent route_plan_2 handling_agent_1)
(prepare_route_for_compliance_without_alternate route_plan_2 equipment_1 handling_agent_1 container_2 transport_leg_1)
(assign_regulatory_clearance_to_route route_plan_2 clearance_1)
(apply_route_clearance route_plan_2 clearance_1 container_2 transport_leg_1)
(finalize_route_without_service_requirements route_plan_2)
(release_vehicle_equipment_from_route route_plan_2 equipment_1)
(release_route_clearance route_plan_2 clearance_1)
(release_handling_agent route_plan_2 handling_agent_1)
(assign_dispatch_window route_plan_2 dispatch_window_1)
(assign_entity_to_delivery_slot route_plan_2 delivery_slot_1 dispatch_window_1)
(deploy_route_and_release_resources route_plan_2 mode_van delivery_slot_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves deploying vehicle_1, driver_1, route_plan_1, and route_plan_2. All must be deployed_integrated.


## Examining deployment needs  
- The task requires deploying vehicle_1, driver_1, route_plan_1, and route_plan_2 together in an integrated manner.  
- All components must work cohesively as part of the deployment process.  

## Assessing available resources  
- The initial state includes various resources like modes, dispatch window, and handling agent.  
- These resources seem ready to support the deployment, but their exact roles need clarification.

## Assessing available resources  
- The system includes additional resources like cargo, zones, containers, permits, and delivery slots, alongside equipment and priority tags.  
- Some resources, such as priority_tag_2, appear in object lists but aren't initially available, which may require special handling.  

## Examining resource relations  
- Route_plan_1 and route_plan_2 both connect to vehicle_1, driver_1, and transport_leg_1, but link to different containers (c1 for rp1, c2 for rp2).  
- Priority bindings exist for both route plans (rp1-pt1, rp2-pt2), but priority_tag_2 isn't available initially, posing a potential deployment issue.  

## Exploring deployment process  
- Deploying vehicle_1, driver_1, route_plan_1, and route_plan_2 requires integrated steps like initialization, mode assignment, and slot binding, up to deployment and release.  
- Shared resources like dispatch window, handling agent, and delivery slot (only one each) suggest a need for sequential processing, possibly deploying and releasing resources like the slot between uses.  

## Identifying sequencing needs  
- Vehicle_1 and driver_1 already have zone assignments, while route plans need finalization steps like finalize_route_with_priority to be ready for deployment.  
- Actions like confirm_vehicle_deployment and finalize_staging involve staging resources, which may require releasing and reassigning shared items like cargo_unit_1.

## Exploring initialization steps  
- The deployment process begins with initializing all components (vehicle_1, driver_1, route_plan_1, route_plan_2) using the initialize_task action, setting them to an "init" state.  
- This initial step requires that components are neither already initialized nor deployed, clearing the way for further assignment actions.

## Examining mode and dispatch assignments  
- Three modes (mode_van, mode_bike, mode_e_cargo) are available, and entities can share modes, with deployment releasing them for reuse, allowing four entities to share three modes.  
- Only one dispatch window exists, so it must be assigned to one entity at a time, activated, and later released for reassignment to others, ensuring sequential processing.

## Investigating staging and readiness processes  
- Staging involves signaling zones as ready (e.g., signal_origin_zone_ready for vehicle_1 at origin1 with dispatch window), setting origin_ready without using cargo, and then staging the vehicle to set stage_flag and ready_flag.  
- Alternative staging with cargo (stage_origin_cargo, then finalize_vehicle_staging) sets origin_staged and requires cargo assignment, but simpler cases can avoid cargo by using signal and stage actions directly.  

## Analyzing leg reservation variants  
- Standard leg reservation requires both origin and destination zones ready, plus vehicle and driver ready flags, without needing staged zones, making it suitable for basic deployment.  
- Other variants (with_midpoint, with_alternate, with_both) involve staged zones and specific readiness conditions, but standard reservation aligns with the current setup without cargo.

## Investigating dispatch window management  
- After staging the driver (d1) and assigning dispatch window (dw1), it can be released from d1, making dw1 available again.  
- The dispatch window can then be reassigned to vehicle_1 (v1) for actions like activate_leg_for_loading, ensuring v1 has the required time assignment.  

## Examining leg activation process  
- Activating a leg for loading (activate_leg_for_loading) requires the leg to be reserved, vehicle staged, and the vehicle assigned a dispatch window, without needing the vehicle to be active.  
- Once activated, the leg is ready for further deployment steps like confirm_vehicle_deployment, which checks staging flags, readiness, and loading status.  

## Exploring route preparation steps  
- Routes (rp1, rp2) need activation, requiring mode selection and dispatch window assignment, with rp1 already set to mode_e_cargo.  
- Additional route tasks include assigning containers, preparing compliance, and handling equipment, permits, and clearances, all dependent on route activation.

## Managing vehicle and driver staging  
- Vehicle_1 is assigned dispatch_window_1, activated, and staged at origin_zone_1 with handling_agent_1 after signaling readiness, then resources like handling_agent_1 and dispatch_window_1 are released.  
- Driver_1 follows a similar process, assigned dispatch_window_1, activated, and staged at destination_zone_1, with resources released afterward, ensuring both are ready for leg reservation.  

## Handling leg reservation and activation  
- With both vehicle_1 and driver_1 staged, transport_leg_1 is reserved, binding origin and destination zones, requiring dispatch_window_1 reassignment to vehicle_1 for activation.  
- Leg activation for loading is completed by reassigning dispatch_window_1, then released again, preparing for deployment confirmation for both vehicle and driver.  

## Finalizing route preparation  
- Route_plan_1, already with mode_e_cargo, is activated by assigning dispatch_window_1, then container_1 is assigned and materialized for transport_leg_1, marking it ready.  
- Equipment, handling agents, and regulatory clearances are assigned to route_plan_1, preparing it for compliance without alternate requirements, and finalized without service needs, achieving ready_final status.  

## Managing route resource allocation  
- Equipment, clearances, and handling agents assigned to route_plan_1 need release actions like release_vehicle_equipment_from_route for reuse, requiring prior binding to make them available.  
- This ensures resources can be reassigned to other routes like route_plan_2, which hasn’t been processed yet and shares similar preparation needs.

## Sequencing task initialization  
- Tasks for vehicle_1, driver_1, route_plan_1, and route_plan_2 are initialized first, setting the stage for further assignments.  
- Transport modes are assigned: vehicle_1 as mode_van, driver_1 as mode_bike, and route_plan_1 as mode_e_cargo, with route_plan_2 pending.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
